texto: It's as if our being academic equals means we must be inadequate wives and mothers,' says Noura.All of these girls have been called 'intimidating' and 'outspoken' by their Muslim counterparts, simply for being themselves. Their accomplishments usually leave men feeling emasculated, they say.'It's still going to take Muslim men a couple more generations to catch up and realise girls like us want love, not money,' says Ayesha.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sabrina Mahfouz: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “It's as if our being academic equals means we must be inadequate wives and mothers,' says Noura.A…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, dating, marriage pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e marriage.

Their accomplishments usually leave men feeling emasculated, they say.'It's still going to take Muslim men a couple more generations to catch up and realise girls like us want love, not money,' says Ayesha.
